content.full_text:
Farn Pollrrcal PnacrrcEs ColrurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 18,2005
Carla Wardlow, Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
4281Street, Ste.620
Sacramento, CA95814
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-05-173
Dear Ms. Wardlow:
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 'Act") and the provisions of
regulation 1 8329 (c)(l) (copy enclosed). I
QUESTION
May political party committees combine the October 31,2005, odd-year report
with the pre-election statements due in connection with the September 13 and
November 8,2005, special state elections?
CONCLUSION
In connection with the September 13 and November 8,2005, elections, political
party committees may use a filing schedule that combines the October 3l,2O0i,odd-year
report with the pre-election statements filed in connection with the elections.
' Government Code sections 81000 - gl)l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations. Informal assistance does noip.ovide the requestor
with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 831 14; regulation nZZO1c1.1


File No. I-05-173
Page No. 2
Effective September 27,2004, section 84200.5 of the Political Reform Act was
amended to require state and county political party committees to file pre-election
campaign statements in connection with a state election if the commitGe receives
contributions totaling $1,000 or more or makes contributions or independent expenditures
totaling $500 or more during the period covered by the pre-election siatement. (Section
84200.5(D.) In addition, section 84202.7 requires all recipient committees to file an
"odd-year report" on October 31 of each odd-numbered year for the period July 1 through
September 30, if contributions totaling $ 10,000 or more are made to state officeholders,-
their controlled committees, or committees primarily formed to support or oppose any
elected state o fficeholder.
Two special state elections will be held in2005, one on September l3 to fill a
vacancy in the 53'd Assembly District, and one on November 8, which is also the date
scheduled for a runoff election if one is needed to fiII the vacant Assembly seat. As a
result, during the period covered by the october 31,2005,odd-year,"pori (July 1
through September 30,2005), political party committees may bL required to file up to
four pre-election statements pursuant to section 54200.5(0.
Section 84205 states:
The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required to be
filed by this title.
Pursuant to section 84205, we advise that if a political party committee discloses
all contributions that would trigger the October 31,2005, odd-yeai report on one or more
of the pre-election statements required in connection with the September 13 and
November 8, 2005, elections, the pre-election statement(s) and the odd-year report may
be combined and no separate odd-year report will be required.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
ANALYSIS
General Counsel
